title: psorospermum membranaceum
intro: Psorospermum membranaceum, also known as quinine tree, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant contains alkaloids with medicinal properties, particularly quinine, which has been used to treat malaria and other diseases. The health benefits of quinine include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and combat the symptoms of malaria. Quinine is often extracted from the plant and prepared as an herbal remedy in the form of powder, tablets, or infusion, although its use should be supervised by a medical professional due to potential side effects.
